# Act 3: Enforcing Tools {.section .focus-section}

<p class="section-subtitle">How Protopipe keeps shared artifacts present, executable, and open to validation.</p>

---

## 14. Purpose and Culture at Runtime

<p class="slide-kicker">Purpose only matters when it is present at the moment a decision becomes work.</p>

<div class="culture-runtime">
<div class="culture-bpmn-block write-only">
<div class="culture-bpmn-heading">
<span>Write-only culture</span>
<strong>Intent is communicated once, then disappears from the work.</strong>
</div>
<img src="../assets/diagrams/write-only-culture-bpmn.svg" alt="Write-only culture BPMN process with separate Leadership and Employee processes">
</div>

<div class="culture-bpmn-block runtime">
<div class="culture-bpmn-heading">
<span>Noesis culture runtime</span>
<strong>Canonical context travels with the decision and learns from its outcome.</strong>
</div>
<img src="../assets/diagrams/noesis-culture-runtime-bpmn.svg" alt="Noesis culture runtime BPMN process across Leadership, Noesis, and Employee lanes">
</div>
</div>

<div class="references">
Applied reading: Alwin Mark, <a href="https://onlu.ch/noesis-wie-metaprompts-unternehmenskultur-in-jede-entscheidung-bringen/">Noesis: Wie Metaprompts Unternehmenskultur in jede Entscheidung bringen</a>, ONLU AG.
</div>

---

## 15. Culture at the Point of Work

<p class="slide-kicker">Noesis turns canonical culture into concrete guidance inside everyday artifacts.</p>

<div class="culture-examples">
<div class="culture-example">
<span>Customer communication</span>
<strong>Does this response express our purpose and shared language?</strong>
The agent reviews wording, commitments, and claims against canonical culture.
</div>
<div class="culture-example">
<span>Ticket / Definition of Done</span>
<strong>Is the work measurable, observable, and connected to a hypothesis?</strong>
The agent proposes missing acceptance criteria, feature scenarios, and learning signals.
</div>
</div>

<div class="culture-punchline">
<span>Remember</span>
Culture becomes operational when shared intent is present in every decision, review, and learning loop.
</div>

---

## 16. Noesis: Start Small, Scale the Governance

<p class="slide-kicker">Noesis is a canonical reasoning space, not a platform commitment. Start with files; add automation when coordination demands it.</p>

<div class="noesis-levels">
<div class="noesis-level minimal">
<span>Low tech / low commitment</span>
<strong>Structured Markdown in Git</strong>
<ul>
<li>Purpose, language, culture, principles, and constraints as readable files</li>
<li>Folders, templates, front matter, and links provide structure</li>
<li>Pull requests make canonical changes explicit and reviewable</li>
<li>Humans and local AI use the same repository as context</li>
</ul>
<div class="noesis-stack">Markdown + Git + search + optional local LLM</div>
</div>

<div class="noesis-level enterprise">
<span>Enterprise runtime</span>
<strong>MCP + Continuous Knowledge Curation</strong>
<ul>
<li>MCP exposes approved context to agents and enterprise tools</li>
<li>Policies control scope, access, provenance, and versioning</li>
<li><b>AutoAudit</b> detects stale, duplicate, orphaned, or contradictory content</li>
<li>Curation proposes merges, links, taxonomy changes, and structural cleanup</li>
</ul>
<div class="noesis-oss-note">Planned as part of the OSS Protopipe package.</div>
<div class="noesis-stack">Knowledge graph + MCP + policy + AutoAudit</div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="noesis-governance">
Automation proposes. Accountable humans approve changes to canonical knowledge.
</div>

---

## 17. Protopipe Cockpit: Strategy Meets Reality

<p class="slide-kicker">The cockpit makes strategy, investment, evidence, and friction visible in one C-level decision surface.</p>

<div class="cockpit-map">
<div class="cockpit-panel health">
<span>01 · Overall Health</span>
<strong>Is the portfolio moving in the intended direction?</strong>
<div class="cockpit-health-score">
<b>72</b>
<div>Momentum <strong>+12%</strong><br>Healthy strategies <strong>4 / 5</strong><br>Capacity <strong>100%</strong></div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="cockpit-panel signals">
<span>02 · Reality Signal Matrix</span>
<strong>Where does reality support or contradict strategy?</strong>
<div class="cockpit-quadrants">
<i>Internal + controllable</i>
<i>Internal + uncontrollable</i>
<i>External + controllable</i>
<i>External + uncontrollable</i>
</div>
</div>

<div class="cockpit-panel strategies">
<span>03 · Strategy Tiles</span>
<strong>Which strategies require attention, budget, or intervention?</strong>
<div class="cockpit-strategy-list">
<i>Enterprise Expansion</i>
<i>Profitability First</i>
<i>Market Resilience</i>
</div>
</div>

<div class="cockpit-panel detail">
<span>04 · Strategy Detail</span>
<strong>What must an executive know before deciding?</strong>
<div class="cockpit-detail-list">
<i>Budget and capacity</i>
<i>Primary friction</i>
<i>Largest open assumption</i>
<i>Most critical risk</i>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="cockpit-punchline">
The cockpit does not report activity. It forces strategy to remain connected to observable reality.
</div>

---

## 18. Cockpit in Action {data-state="cockpit-demo-slide"}

<p class="slide-kicker">From the operating model to an executable executive decision surface.</p>

<div class="cockpit-demo-frame">
<iframe
  src="../assets/mockups/ProtopipeCockpit.html"
  title="Interactive Protopipe Executive Strategy Cockpit"
  loading="lazy">
</iframe>
</div>

---

## 19. Proto-Portfolio: Strategy-Linked Experimentation

::: {.diagram-slot}
  Proto Portfolio connects strategy to experiments by making strategic hypotheses explicit, measurable, and continuously observable.
:::

---

## 20. ProtoCatalog: One Behavior Model

<p class="slide-kicker">Requirements, specifications, implementation, and tests drift when each role maintains a different model of the product.</p>

<div class="catalog-model">
<div class="catalog-fragments">
<div class="catalog-fragment">
<span>Product</span>
<strong>Acceptance Criteria</strong>
<small>What we expect</small>
</div>
<div class="catalog-fragment">
<span>Analysis</span>
<strong>Specification</strong>
<small>What we mean</small>
</div>
<div class="catalog-fragment">
<span>Development</span>
<strong>Implementation</strong>
<small>What the system does</small>
</div>
<div class="catalog-fragment">
<span>Quality</span>
<strong>Test Suite</strong>
<small>What is verified</small>
</div>
</div>

<div class="catalog-convergence" aria-hidden="true">→</div>

<div class="catalog-contract">
<span>ProtoCatalog</span>
<strong>Feature Files + Scenarios</strong>
<p>One versioned behavior model, written in domain language and executable through Cucumber.</p>
<div class="catalog-contract-properties">
<i>Readable by business</i>
<i>Executable by development</i>
<i>Verified by evidence</i>
<i>Traceable to the experiment</i>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="catalog-punchline">
BDD is the communication protocol. The catalog is the living product knowledge it creates.
</div>

<p class="source-note">Based on the Noesis feature model and <em>From spec to BDD</em>, Michael Küsters and Finies Fenske, 2018.</p>

---

## 21. Progress Becomes Observable

<p class="slide-kicker">A feature branch is visible in the catalog while it belongs to an experiment and its strategic hypothesis.</p>

<div class="catalog-trace">
<div class="catalog-trace-context">
<div class="catalog-trace-node focus">
<span>Strategy</span>
<strong>Increase checkout completion</strong>
</div>
<div class="catalog-trace-arrow">→</div>
<div class="catalog-trace-node">
<span>Hypothesis</span>
<strong>Clear payment feedback reduces abandonment</strong>
</div>
<div class="catalog-trace-arrow">→</div>
<div class="catalog-trace-node experiment">
<span>Experiment</span>
<strong>EXP-024 · Payment confirmation</strong>
</div>
</div>

<div class="catalog-branch">
<div class="catalog-branch-head">
<div>
<span>Feature branch</span>
<strong>experiment/EXP-024-payment-feedback</strong>
</div>
<b>5 / 8 steps green</b>
</div>

<div class="catalog-feature">
<div class="catalog-feature-head">
<span>Feature</span>
<strong>Customer receives reliable payment confirmation</strong>
</div>
<div class="catalog-scenario">
<span>Scenario · Successful card payment</span>
<i class="green">Given an active basket</i>
<i class="green">When payment is accepted</i>
<i class="green">Then the order is placed</i>
<i class="green">And confirmation is shown</i>
</div>
<div class="catalog-scenario">
<span>Scenario · Delayed provider response</span>
<i class="green">Given payment remains pending</i>
<i class="failed">When the provider times out</i>
<i class="open">Then retry guidance is shown</i>
<i class="open">And the event remains observable</i>
</div>
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="catalog-status-legend">
<i class="green">Green</i>
<i class="failed">Not successful</i>
<i class="open">Not implemented</i>
<strong>A feature is green when all of its steps are verified.</strong>
</div>

---

## 22. Composer: Architecture as an Experiment System

<p class="slide-kicker">Composer creates an experimental boundary inside the existing system before a migration program exists.</p>

<div class="composer-flow">
<div class="composer-stage legacy">
<span>01 · Install and mark</span>
<strong>Composer becomes the legacy proxy</strong>
<div class="composer-marker">
<code>&lt;!-- protopipe:slot checkout-cta --&gt;</code>
<code>  &lt;button&gt;Buy now&lt;/button&gt;</code>
<code>&lt;!-- /protopipe:slot --&gt;</code>
</div>
<p>Composer proxies the unchanged monolith response. HTML markers identify the first replaceable surface without a page rewrite.</p>
<em>Planned bootstrap adapter</em>
</div>

<div class="composer-arrow">→</div>

<div class="composer-stage experiment">
<span>02 · Replace with artifacts</span>
<strong>Route variants, not conditions</strong>
<div class="composer-variants">
<i><b>Control</b>Legacy fragment</i>
<i><b>Variant</b>RFA from feature branch</i>
</div>
<p>Composer assigns consenting users and replaces only the expected RFA within the experiment scope.</p>
<em>Artifact-based experiment</em>
</div>

<div class="composer-arrow">→</div>

<div class="composer-stage evidence">
<span>03 · Observe and decide</span>
<strong>Make impact and safety visible</strong>
<div class="composer-signals">
<i>Experiment analytics</i>
<i>Operational telemetry</i>
<i>Runtime context</i>
<i>Deterministic rendering</i>
</div>
<p>Business effects and technical reliability remain separate, observable, and attributable to the variant.</p>
<em>Evidence returns to the experiment</em>
</div>
</div>

<div class="composer-zero-one">
<span>Install Composer as legacy proxy</span>
<b>→</b>
<span>Mark one legacy surface</span>
<b>→</b>
<span>Deploy one RFA</span>
<b>→</b>
<strong>0 → 1 experiment in &lt; 1 week — potentially one day</strong>
</div>

<p class="source-note">Derived from the Composer BDD feature suite: routing, partial rendering, scoped RFA overrides, assignment, consent, runtime context, analytics separation, and operational telemetry.</p>

---

## 23. Storybook: UX Communication Layer

::: {.toc-only}
Marketing, prototype generation, contracts, and future WYSIWYG assembly.
:::

---

## 24. Component Responsibilities

| Component | Primary Job | Failure It Prevents |
|---|---|---|
| Noesis | Keep shared meaning stable | Semantic drift |
| Cockpit | Keep strategy connected to reality | Strategy detachment |
| ProtoPortfolio | Connect strategy to measurable hypotheses | Activity steering |
| ProtoCatalog | Make expected behavior explicit and testable | Invisible assumptions |
| Composer | Make architecture experimentally changeable | Big upfront commitments |
| Storybook | Make experience inspectable and negotiable | Invisible UX assumptions |

---

## 25. How Shared Artifacts Stay Alive

<p class="slide-kicker">The artifact defines shared meaning. The tooling keeps it connected, executable, and open to validation.</p>

<div class="artifact-flow enforcement">
<div class="artifact-card">
<span>Purpose</span>
<strong>Golden Circle</strong>
The canonical why, how, and what remain available as organizational context.
<div class="artifact-tool">
<b>Enforced by</b>
Noesis
</div>
</div>

<div class="artifact-card focus">
<span>Strategy</span>
<strong>Strategy Topic + KPI Tree</strong>
Strategic intent remains connected to measurable outcomes and portfolio governance.
<div class="artifact-tool">
<b>Enforced by</b>
Protopipe Cockpit
</div>
</div>

<div class="artifact-card experiment">
<span>Hypothesis -> Experiment</span>
<strong>Strategy-linked Experiment</strong>
Every experiment names the hypothesis, expected metric movement, and accountable owner.
<div class="artifact-tool">
<b>Enforced by</b>
ProtoPortfolio
</div>
</div>

<div class="artifact-card experiment">
<span>Experiment -> Development</span>
<strong>Feature Files + Scenarios</strong>
Expected behavior becomes an executable contract and progress becomes visible as remaining steps.
<div class="artifact-tool">
<b>Enforced by</b>
ProtoCatalog
</div>
</div>

<div class="artifact-card learning">
<span>Observation -> Learning</span>
<strong>Evidence + Decision Record</strong>
Runtime signals validate the expected effect and update canonical organizational knowledge.
<div class="artifact-tool">
<b>Enforced by</b>
Composer
</div>
</div>
</div>

<div class="artifact-punchline learning">
Shared reality survives daily operations because every artifact has a system that keeps it current and testable.
</div>
